Title: Lipid transfer from plants to arbuscular mycorrhiza fungi

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 16:0 β-monoacylglycerol / plant-derived lipid transferred to fungus; fungal 16:1ω5 fatty acid-containing lipids
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Periarbuscular membrane (discussed/hypothesized for STR/STR2 based on prior work)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis colonization; arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis; [U-13C6]glucose feeding
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; lipid profiling; MAG profiling; 13C isotopolog profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal arbuscule branching, vesicle formation, AM signature lipid accumulation, 16:0 β-MAG abundance, and transfer of labeled lipid from plant to fungus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. str mutants phenocopy dis and ram2 in reduced colonization, defective arbuscule branching, loss of fungal vesicles, and reduced AM signature lipids. 2. str mutants show reduced 16:0 β-MAG and fail to transfer 13C-labeled fatty acid to the fungus. 3. STR is proposed as part of the lipid transport machinery across the periarbuscular membrane, though substrate identity remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colonized roots/arbuscules and associated fungal structures
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports transfer of host lipids needed for fungal membrane/storage lipid formation and AM development
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function strongly disrupts symbiosis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics; MAG profiling; isotopolog profiling; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport role is strongly suggested but not definitively proven in this paper; likely acts with STR2 as a full transporter

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: AM-induced in colonized roots; induction lost in ram1 mutants
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in wild type and ram1 mutants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes precursor supply for AM-specific C16 fatty acid synthesis; AM induction depends on RAM1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KASIII transcript is induced during AM colonization in wild type roots. 2. This induction is abolished in ram1 mutants, placing KASIII in the RAM1-controlled lipid biosynthesis module. 3. The gene is discussed as producing C4 precursors for DIS-mediated elongation to 16:0.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports synthesis of 16:0 fatty acids used for lipid transfer to AM fungi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single copy gene in Lotus according to BLAST analysis; not a primary mutant analyzed in this study

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: AM-induced in colonized roots; induction not affected by RAM1 deficiency
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in wild type and ram1 mutants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as comparison gene showing RAM1 specificity for C16-oriented AM lipid module rather than all FA elongation genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KASII elongates C16 to C18 acyl chains. 2. Unlike DIS and KASIII, its AM induction is not impaired in ram1 mutants. 3. This supports the specificity of RAM1 regulation for the AM lipid transfer pathway.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: General fatty acid elongation; comparator for pathway specificity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single copy gene in Lotus; included for pathway specificity, not as a central experimentally manipulated gene

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KASI condensing enzyme; plastid fatty acid synthase component with plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: AM-specific fatty acid and lipid biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid de novo C16 fatty acid synthesis module in arbuscocytes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: successive condensation reactions during fatty acyl chain elongation from C4:0-ACP to C16:0-ACP
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: acyl-ACP intermediates from C4:0-ACP onward; malonyl-ACP
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream to downstream, C16-producing step of plastid FA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key AM-specific control step for supplying 16:0 precursors; rate-limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in colonized roots; predominantly active in arbuscocytes and also in cells with pre-penetration apparatus; some cortical expression in non-colonized root segments
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native DIS promoter is AM-inducible; PT4 promoter used for arbuscocyte-specific complementation; Ubiquitin promoter used for localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis colonization; arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis; [U-13C6]glucose feeding in isotopolog experiments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Forward genetics/map-based cloning; allelic mutants (dis-1, dis-4); transgenic complementation; arbuscocyte-specific complementation with PT4 promoter; cross-species complementation with Arabidopsis KASI; lipid profiling; 13C isotopolog profiling; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for arbuscule branching, vesicle formation, normal root colonization, AM-specific accumulation of 16:0/16:1ω5 lipid species, 16:0 β-MAG production, and transfer of plant-derived lipid to the fungus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DIS encodes an AM-specific KASI paralog required for normal arbuscule branching, vesicle formation, and quantitative colonization. 2. DIS supplies 16:0 fatty acid precursors in arbuscocytes; dis mutants lose the AM-specific increase of 16:0- and 16:1ω5-containing TAGs/phospholipids and show reduced 16:0 β-MAG. 3. DIS function is enzymatically equivalent to canonical Arabidopsis KASI, but its AM-specific role derives from AM-induced expression. 4. dis mutants fail to transfer labeled fatty acid from plant to fungus in 13C isotopolog profiling, supporting a causal role in interkingdom lipid transfer.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colonized roots/arbuscocytes; associated fungal intraradical and extraradical structures
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal membrane/storage lipid formation and AM fungal growth/development during symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Visible plant growth defects were not observed in dis mutants under tested conditions, but disrupting the gene strongly impairs fungal development and colonization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Next-generation resequencing for mutant identification; lipidomics; isotopolog profiling; qRT-PCR; no accession reported for resequencing dataset in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AM-specific paralog found mainly in AM-host dicotyledons and a gymnosperm; not induced in non-colonized roots except limited cortical signal; likely acts in same pathway as FatM and RAM2

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: AM-specific fatty acid and lipid biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol backbone to produce β-monoacylglycerol with preference for 16:0 fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 16:0 fatty acids (or 16:0 acyl donor) and glycerol-3-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream of DIS in AM-specific lipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms candidate transferred lipid / immediate precursor to transferred lipid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key downstream step in AM-specific lipid production; decisive terminal biosynthetic step for 16:0 β-MAG candidate not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in colonized roots; promoter activity strictly associated with arbuscocytes and cells during PPA/arbuscule development
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native RAM2 promoter is AM-inducible; PT4 promoter used for arbuscocyte-specific complementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis colonization; arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis; [U-13C6]glucose feeding in isotopolog experiments
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Forward genetics/map-based cloning/Sanger sequencing; allelic mutants (ram2-1, ram2-2); transgenic complementation; arbuscocyte-specific complementation with PT4 promoter; MAG profiling; lipid profiling; 13C isotopolog profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for arbuscule branching, vesicle formation, normal root colonization, 16:0 β-MAG accumulation, characteristic AM lipid accumulation, and plant-to-fungus lipid transfer
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAM2 is the Lotus ortholog of Medicago RAM2 and is essential for normal arbuscule branching, vesicle formation, and colonization. 2. Colonized ram2 roots specifically lose 16:0 β-MAG and overaccumulate 16:0-containing phospholipids/TAGs, indicating that RAM2 acts downstream of DIS and consumes DIS-derived 16:0 fatty acids. 3. RAM2 is required for transfer of 13C-labeled fatty acid from plant to fungus, supporting 16:0 β-MAG or a derived lipid as the transported form. 4. Arbuscocyte-specific RAM2 expression is sufficient to restore AM development.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports lipid delivery to AM fungi for fungal development and accumulation of fungal signature 16:1ω5 lipids
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious whole-plant growth defect reported, but mutation strongly impairs fungal development and redirects 16:0 into host phospholipid/storage lipid pools
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics; MAG profiling; qRT-PCR; isotopolog profiling; no omics accession reported for Lotus RAM2 experiments
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter sequence accession KX823335 reported; product is inferred/predicted β-MAG with preference for 16:0 and 18:1, but transported species could be β-MAG or a downstream derivative

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 16:0 β-monoacylglycerol; AM signature lipids including fungal 16:1ω5 fatty acid-containing lipids
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: AM-specific lipid biosynthesis and lipid transfer module in arbuscocytes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or is required for AM-dependent induction of DIS and KASIII; previously shown to mediate expression of RAM2 and STR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master AM regulatory module component controlling activation of arbuscocyte lipid biosynthesis/transport genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis colonization; arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible broader AM-specific regulatory module; explicit feedback not established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (ram1-3, ram1-4); qRT-PCR for downstream genes; lipid profiling; MAG profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of RAM1 impairs AM-specific lipid accumulation and reduces 16:0 β-MAG through failure to induce lipid biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAM1 is required for AM-dependent induction of DIS and KASIII, but not KASII. 2. ram1 mutants show reduced AM signature lipids and reduced 16:0 β-MAG, consistent with upstream control of the lipid transfer pathway. 3. RAM1 therefore defines an AM-specific regulatory module coordinating lipid production and delivery to the fungus.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colonized roots/arbuscocytes and associated fungal structures
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal development during AM symbiosis through activation of host lipid supply
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes strong AM defects; overexpression utility not tested and may have pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; lipidomics; MAG profiling; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding to target promoters was not demonstrated in this paper; regulatory role partly supported by prior studies cited
